children.text: Greens Farms has a calmer, more established cadence than downtown Westport. It’s one of the areas where Westport feels more purely residential, with a coastal proximity that’s part of the lifestyle even if you’re not directly on the water.

Real estate is predominantly single-family, and many buyers like Greens Farms for the balance: quiet streets and an understated feel, with easy access to town amenities when you want restaurants, shopping, or community events.

Commuter access is a key advantage. Green’s Farms station is on the Metro-North New Haven Line; the MTA notes two ticket machines on the westbound platform just outside the station building and no ticket office. That proximity to rail makes the neighborhood especially practical for NYC-bound commuters. Greens Farms is best for buyers who want a quieter Westport setting with train access close by and shoreline amenities within easy reach.

highlights: Green's Farms Metro-North station (New Haven Line) for commuter rail access
A quieter, established Westport neighborhood with coastal proximity
Easy access to downtown Westport and the Saugatuck area for dining and shopping
Strong fit for buyers who want a residential setting with rail convenience
Beautiful landscapes and a peaceful suburban atmosphere
name: Greens Farms
overview: Greens Farms is a historic coastal area of Westport with a quieter, established feel and strong rail convenience. Homes are primarily single-family, and the lifestyle balances residential calm with easy access to the shoreline and downtown Westport. The Green’s Farms Metro-North station is a major practical asset on the New Haven Line, making commuting feasible without living in the busiest part of town. For buyers who want Westport’s coastal side with train access nearby, Greens Farms is a strong fit.
